candidates.name: iso-639-5
candidates.description: ISO 639-5 (language families) on top of existing iso-codes.
candidates.notes: iso-codes covers 639-1/639-2/639-3; this is the family bucket.

candidates.name: lei
candidates.notes: 20 alphanumeric chars + mod-97 check; small.

candidates.name: vat
candidates.description: VAT number validation (EU + common non-EU formats).
candidates.notes: Per-country checksum rules; tedious but well-defined.

candidates.name: flatbuffers
candidates.notes: Same schema problem as protobuf.

candidates.name: json5
candidates.notes: Tolerant of comments, trailing commas, unquoted keys.

candidates.name: protobuf
candidates.notes: Schema interface is the hard part; flatbuffers + thrift have the same shape.

candidates.name: tar
candidates.notes: zipfile exists; tar is the unix analog.

candidates.name: thrift
candidates.notes: Schema problem; less common than protobuf.

candidates.name: wasm-introspect
candidates.notes: Meta: query the catalog's own components via SQL.

candidates.name: yaml
candidates.description: YAML parse/serialize as scalars (yaml-to-json handler exists; this is SQL-side).
candidates.notes: handlers/yaml-to-json covers the httpd side; SQL scalar form is missing.

candidates.name: aes-modes
candidates.notes: aead covers GCM + ChaCha20-Poly1305; CBC/CTR/SIV for legacy + deterministic AEAD.

candidates.name: cose
candidates.notes: CBOR-shaped JOSE alternative; used by WebAuthn / FIDO2.

candidates.name: ed25519
candidates.description: Standalone Ed25519 sign/verify scalars (separate from jwt).
candidates.notes: jwt has Ed25519 internally; exposing it as plain scalars is its own item.

candidates.name: hpke
candidates.notes: Modern asymmetric encryption suite; pairs with aead + hkdf.

candidates.name: oauth-pkce
candidates.notes: Tiny surface; could fold into a future auth-helpers ext.

candidates.name: pgp
candidates.notes: sequoia-openpgp is too heavy; pgp crate is the lighter choice.

candidates.name: rsa
candidates.description: RSA sign/verify/encrypt/decrypt with PKCS#1 + OAEP padding.
candidates.notes: jwt currently lacks RS256 (deferred to v2); this would close that loop.

candidates.name: sha1
candidates.notes: sha3 + blake3 + hashes-fast already in tree; sha1 still needed for git/legacy.

candidates.name: webauthn
candidates.notes: Heavy spec; needs handler-side surface as well as SQL.

candidates.name: x25519
candidates.description: X25519 elliptic curve Diffie-Hellman key exchange (RFC 7748).
candidates.notes: Standalone curve for ECDH; ed25519-dalek is already pulled in by jwt. Sister to existing secp256k1.

candidates.name: lru-cache
candidates.description: LRU cache as a vtab + scalars.
candidates.notes: Useful for materializing expensive computed columns.

candidates.name: priority-queue
candidates.description: Heap-backed priority queue as a vtab.
candidates.notes: Generic; might fold into a future data-structures family.

candidates.name: skiplist
candidates.notes: roaring covers exact set; skiplist is sorted-set.

candidates.name: mathml
candidates.notes: Niche; specialized for scientific document workflows.

candidates.name: geohash
candidates.notes: h3 + s2 + mgrs in catalog; geohash is the popular short-string scheme.

candidates.name: polyline-simplify
candidates.notes: Pairs with google-polyline + h3/s2.

candidates.name: proj
candidates.notes: Heavy C dependency; defer until needed.

candidates.name: utm
candidates.notes: mgrs uses utm internally; standalone surface would also be useful.

candidates.name: rsa-bignum
candidates.description: Standalone RSA-style bignum modexp + key gen (separate from `rsa` crate).
candidates.notes: bignum has modpow; this would add a key-gen surface specifically.

candidates.name: signal-processing
candidates.description: FIR/IIR filters, autocorrelation, convolution beyond fft.
candidates.notes: fft covers transform; this is the filtering surface.

candidates.name: color-palette
candidates.description: Extract dominant color palette from image blobs.
candidates.notes: Pairs with color + image-meta.

candidates.name: dicom
candidates.notes: Medical / healthcare context.

candidates.name: djvu
candidates.proposed_crate: no crate yet  port djvulibre header
candidates.notes: Niche; defer until a consumer asks.

candidates.name: docx-meta
candidates.notes: OOXML is a zip + XML; might overlap with formats/xml + zipfile.

candidates.name: epub
candidates.notes: image-meta + pdf-meta + id3 + vcard cover other formats; epub is the next.

candidates.name: perceptual-hash
candidates.description: pHash / dHash / aHash for image similarity.
candidates.notes: Needs the image crate for pixel decode  bigger wasm bundle than image-meta.

candidates.name: http-signature
candidates.notes: Used by ActivityPub / Mastodon and the SSF working group.

candidates.name: mqtt-parse
candidates.notes: IoT message parsing.

candidates.name: nmea
candidates.notes: GPS receiver output parsing.

candidates.name: robotstxt
candidates.notes: Web crawling staple; small.

candidates.name: sitemap-xml
candidates.notes: Pairs with robotstxt for crawler use cases.

candidates.name: tld-list
candidates.description: Comprehensive TLD list with metadata (gtld vs cctld, etc).
candidates.notes: publicsuffix covers the PSL; this is the IANA gold-source root zone.

candidates.name: whois-parse
candidates.notes: Many response formats; pick the common ARIN/RIPE shapes.

candidates.name: multi-comparison
candidates.description: Multiple comparison corrections (Bonferroni / Holm / Benjamini-Hochberg FDR).
candidates.notes: Pairs with hypothesis  obvious followup.

candidates.name: hyphenation
candidates.notes: Multi-language; ~50 KB per language pattern file.

candidates.name: lemmatize
candidates.notes: stemmer (Snowball) is morphological; lemma uses a lookup table.

candidates.name: ngrams
candidates.notes: Pairs with stemmer + fuzzy + lang-detect.

candidates.name: pinyin
candidates.notes: Mainland-China-specific; useful for CN-language pipelines.

candidates.name: sentence-split
candidates.description: Sentence boundary detection for arbitrary text.
candidates.notes: Common preprocessing step; non-trivial for non-English.

candidates.name: mbox
candidates.description: mbox + maildir email format parsing as vtab.
candidates.notes: Useful for email-database use cases.

candidates.name: sqlean-vsv
candidates.notes: csv extension exists but vsv has different shape  worth comparing.
